chapters.summary: Covers accounting concepts, principles, and conventions (entity concept, money measurement, going concern, periodicity, accrual, matching, consistency, conservatism, materiality). Also reviews capital vs. revenue expenditures.

keyConcepts.title: Conservatism / Prudence Concept
keyConcepts.description: State that anticipate no profit, but provide for all possible losses. Do not record assets at inflated values, and do record liabilities when probable.

keyConcepts.title: Capital vs. Revenue Expenditure
keyConcepts.description: Capital expenditure yields benefit over multiple accounting periods (e.g., purchasing machinery), while revenue expenditure is consumed in the current period (e.g., repairs, salaries).

formulas.name: Accounting Equation
formulas.description: The fundamental basis of double-entry accounting.

videos.title: CA Foundation Accounting Basic Concepts One Shot

practiceResources.title: ICAI CA Foundation Accounts Study Material Chapter 1

quizzes.question: Which of the following is an example of capital expenditure?
options: Payment of salaries to factory workers
Cost of installing a new machine
quizzes.explanation: The cost of installation is directly attributable to bringing the new machine (asset) to its working condition, thus capitalized as a capital expenditure.
